title: Fixing Node.js Express Middleware Route for Dynamic Route Parameter Validation

statement.markdown: Write a Node.js Express middleware route to validate a dynamic route parameter. The route should accept an HTTP GET request to `/users/:id` and return a JSON response with the user object if the id is valid. If the id is invalid, it should return a 404 status code. However, the existing code does not handle the case where the id is not a positive integer. Fix the code to handle this scenario.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an HTTP GET request to `/users/:id` with the id as a path parameter.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a JSON response with the user object if the id is valid, otherwise a 404 status code.
statement.notes: The user object has an id and a name. You can assume that the user object is stored in a database and can be retrieved by the id.

examples.explanation: User with id 1 exists in the database.
User with id -1 does not exist in the database.
User id is not a positive integer.

hints.text: First hint text: Validate the user id as a positive integer before querying the database.

editorial.title: Editorial title: Fixing Node.js Express Middleware Route for Dynamic Route Parameter Validation
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Validate the user id as a positive integer before querying the database.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Query the database for every possible user id and return a 404 status code if the id is invalid.
editorial.betterSolution: Better solution summary: Validate the user id as a positive integer before querying the database.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Use a regular expression to validate the user id as a positive integer and query the database if the id is valid.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The optimal solution returns the correct user object if the id is valid and a 404 status code if the id is invalid.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Input /users/1, output {"id":1,"name":"John Doe"}.
commonMistakes: Not validating the user id as a positive integer
Not querying the database for the valid user id
